mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in > 65% of triggered instances or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment is already above 7%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and housing starts indicates broad-based economic demand that sustains corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of cases or interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month declines in the industrial production index reflect immediate supply-side bottlenecks that trigger short-term equity sell-offs within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of declining INDPRO observations or the signal reverses direction during liquidity-driven bull markets.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation measured by CPIAUCSL accelerates above 2.5% while the federal funds rate remains below 0.5%, markets price in imminent tightening that compresses equity valuations over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL crosses above 215.0 while FEDFUNDS stays below 0.5
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances or the threshold condition fails to separate winning and losing months with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and wage growth that supports corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows or payroll growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns during stagflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated real savings and financial assets indicate strong household balance sheets that fuel consumer discretionary spending, supporting positive equity returns over the next month during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: Signal predicts negative returns in > 55% of cases or loses predictive power when household debt-to-income ratios exceed 120%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling inflation signals improving supply chain efficiency that reduces input costs and boosts short-term equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: Interaction term yields negative 5-day returns in > 60% of observations or the divergence pattern fails to beat a baseline momentum strategy.
